scope_creep.notes: Topic title boundary: 'Writing a post-mortem — what failed, why, who was accountable, what governance would have prevented it.' All content in sections 5–8 directly teaches the four-section post-mortem framework. Section 7 real-world patterns (SRE, criminal justice, content moderation) apply the framework to domain examples without teaching those domains as independent curriculum concepts. The inline definition of 'AI-assisted risk scoring tools' in the criminal justice paragraph is a one-sentence contextualising descriptor, not a bolded or formally defined curriculum concept. All governance vocabulary (HITL checkpoint, risk-tiered component mapping, triggered oversight assignment, error budget) is applied as prior-topic vocabulary with explicit attribution to topics 10.2–10.6, not re-taught. No material found that teaches beyond the headline scope.

beginner_readability.notes: All non-everyday terms defined on first use: 'post-mortem' defined with Latin etymology and plain meaning in section 4; 'no-blame post-mortem' defined in section 5.1; 'proximate cause' and 'root cause' defined with explicit contrast and concrete examples in section 5.3; 'post-incident review' parenthetically introduced alongside 'post-mortem' in section 5.1; 'SRE (Site Reliability Engineering)' spelled out on first use in section 7. 'HITL' used without re-spell-out in section 8 — permissible as a prior-topic term introduced in topic 10.5 (artifact-mix audience mandate: prior-sequence terms may be reused without re-definition). No jargon dumps detected (no paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously). Sentences are active voice, second-person address throughout, generally under 25 words. No hedging phrases detected. Zero findings; below 3-finding escalation threshold.

verdict: PASS. All six checks pass on this final revision. Required sections are present and substantive. Citation density at ~87% across sections 5+6+7 substantially exceeds the 60% threshold; primary resource [1] is cited throughout; no fabricated citations. No forward references detected — all vocabulary traces to this topic's own concepts_introduced or prior topics 10.1–10.6. Word count ~5020 clears the deep-profile floor of 5000 words (the section 7 expansion addressed the single blocking issue from the previous run). Scope remains tightly bounded to the post-mortem four-section framework. Beginner readability is solid with zero findings. Corpus is cleared to proceed to HITL #2.
